peopleAlsoAsk.answer: In addition to 120–150 credit hours of education, prospective CPAs in NC need to pass all 4 CPA Exam sections, complete a state-specific ethics course, and earn 1 to 2 years of supervised experience. The whole process of becoming a CPA typically takes 6–8 years from the start of college to licensure.
